mnemonics.title: IPv4 Address Classes
mnemonics.content:
A: 1-126 (A single network)
B: 128-191 (BB starting balance)
C: 192-223 (C for cookie jar - 223)
D: 224-239 (D for drama - multicast)
E: 240-255 (E for experimental - future)

acronyms.usage: Supersedes classful addressing, allows variable-length subnet masks (VLSM)
Maps private IPs to public IPs, solves IPv4 address exhaustion
Maps IP addresses to MAC addresses in local network
Sends error messages and operational information (ping, traceroute)
Automatically assigns IP addresses to network devices
IPv6 address auto-configuration without DHCP server
IPv6 replacement for ARP, router discovery, and path MTU discovery
Largest packet size that can be sent over a network without fragmentation

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box divided into rows: Version(4), IHL(4), TOS(8), Total Length(16) | Identification(16), Flags(3), Fragment Offset(13) | TTL(8), Protocol(8), Header Checksum(16) | Source IP(32) | Destination IP(32) | Options(variable)
Simpler layout: Version(4), Traffic Class(8), Flow Label(20) | Payload Length(16), Next Header(8), Hop Limit(8) | Source Address(128) | Destination Address(128)
IPv4 = 32 bits = 4 bytes (like a 4-digit phone number); IPv6 = 128 bits = 16 bytes (like a 16-character password) - IPv6 is 4 times wider but exponentially larger (2^96 times more addresses)
10.x.x.x (big mansion - 1 network with many rooms), 172.16-31.x.x (apartment building - 16 networks), 192.168.x.x (small office - 256 networks)

analogies.analogy: Like running out of unique phone numbers in an area code - you need area code overlays (NAT) or switch to a new numbering system (IPv6)
Like adding attachments to an email - the main email (base header) carries routing info, while attachments (extension headers) provide additional functions like fragmentation or security
Like a food expiration date - each router 'checks' and decrements the counter; when it reaches zero, the packet is 'thrown away' to prevent it from circulating forever
Like being bilingual - a dual-stack device can speak both IPv4 and IPv6 'languages' to communicate with anyone
